title: Specific detection of the SARS-CoV-2 Omicron variant by RT-PCR
author: Corbisier Philippe
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A worldwide outburst of SARS-CoV-2 Omicron (B.1.1.529) indicates that this variant is becoming quickly dominant. As a rapid alternative to sequencing, we present a RT-PCR method for the direct and specific detection of Omicron. The method, designed  in silico,  was validated on clinical samples containing Omicron, using as negative controls synthetic RNA genomes of the isolate Wuhan  Hu  - 1   and of the Alpha (B.1.1.7), Beta (B.1.351), Gamma (P.1), Kappa (B.1.617.1), Iota (B.1.526), Epsilon (B.1.429) and Delta (B.1.617.2) SARS-CoV-2 variants.
steps_raw: A worldwide outburst of SARS-CoV-2 Omicron (B.1.1.529) indicates that this variant is becoming quickly dominant. As a rapid alternative to sequencing, we present a RT-PCR method for the direct and specific detection of Omicron. The method, designed  in silico,  was validated on clinical samples containing Omicron, using as negative controls synthetic RNA genomes of the isolate Wuhan  Hu  - 1   and of the Alpha (B.1.1.7), Beta (B.1.351), Gamma (P.1), Kappa (B.1.617.1), Iota (B.1.526), Epsilon (B.1.429) and Delta (B.1.617.2) SARS-CoV-2 variants.
